name: Bluesky MCP Server
what_it_does: Bluesky (AT Protocol) MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with the Bluesky decentralized social network — reading posts and feeds, searching content, following accounts, posting (with appropriate authorization), and integrating Bluesky social data into agent-driven content monitoring and engagement workflows.
use_cases: Monitoring Bluesky feeds for relevant content from social listening agents
Searching Bluesky posts on specific topics from research agents
Reading user profiles and post history from content analysis agents
Drafting and posting content to Bluesky from social media management agents
Tracking brand mentions and community discussions from PR monitoring agents
not_for: Automated spam or bulk posting (violates Bluesky ToS)
Teams not on Bluesky (use Twitter/X or Mastodon MCPs for other platforms)
best_when: A creator, researcher, or social media manager active on Bluesky needs AI assistance monitoring feeds, tracking discussions, and managing their Bluesky presence.
avoid_when: Your audience is primarily on Twitter/X, Instagram, or other platforms — use those platforms' MCPs. Bluesky has different demographics.

auth.notes: Bluesky app password required (not regular password). Create at bsky.app → Settings → App Passwords. Set BLUESKY_IDENTIFIER (handle) and BLUESKY_APP_PASSWORD environment variables.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Bluesky API is free; rate limits apply for posting
pricing.notes: Bluesky API is free to use. App password auth is free. Rate limits apply. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: AT Protocol rate limits: reading is generous; posting is rate-limited

known_agent_gotchas: Use app passwords, not main password — app passwords are revocable and safer
AT Protocol is still evolving — API changes may break MCP compatibility
Posting requires rate limit awareness — automated posting can trigger spam detection
Bluesky's AT Protocol has unique concepts (DIDs, ATURIs) — understand the data model
Community MCP — test read operations before enabling write/post capabilities
Bluesky moderation policies apply — automated content must comply with ToS
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS. App password (not main password) — safer for API access. App passwords revocable. Social data is public by default on Bluesky. Post actions have public social consequences.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. App password auth. Use revocable app passwords, not main password. Posts are public. Comply with Bluesky ToS for automated use.
